elements.sent-2411-033: Extending Ramsey's theorem in an orthogonal direction, a prominent theme has been to determine the threshold for Ramsey properties in random graphs.
elements.sent-2411-034: Here and throughout, we denote by $\bfg(n,p)$ the binomial random graph with edge probability $p=p(n)$ and we say that a function $\hat p = \hat p(n)$ is the threshold for a monotone increasing graph property $\cP$ if \begin{equation}\lim_{n\to\infty}\Pr(\bfg(n,p) satisfies \cP) = 0 if p =o( \hat p), 1 if p =\omega( \hat p). \end{equation} We refer to $\hat p$ as the threshold for $\cP$, although it is not uniquely defined but rather defined up to a choice of constant.
